notes: Maps Apple A-numbers to canonical scraper model family labels (matching BackMarket product page titles exposed via V7 scraper). Source-of-truth for bm-grade-check and listings-audit.

A2179.conflict_resolved: bm-grade-check hard-coded the non-canonical V7 family string "Air 13\" 2020 Intel i5 Grey", but sell-prices-latest.json exposes no 2020 Intel Air family label. The shared map keeps the Apple A-number metadata and disables scraper fallback until a canonical V7 label exists.

A2681.conflict_resolved: listings-audit incorrectly reused A2681 for MacBook Pro 13-inch M2 titles. Apple identifies A2681 as the 13-inch MacBook Air chassis, and the exact V7 family label is Air 13" 2022 M2.

A3113.conflict_resolved: Apple identifies A3113 as MacBook Air 13-inch M3. This resolves the drift between the repo's older A3114/A3211 assumptions and the exact V7 family label Air 13" 2024 M3.

A3114.conflict_resolved: bm-grade-check mapped A3114 to Air 13" 2025 M4, but Apple identifies A3114 as MacBook Air 15-inch M3. sell-prices-latest.json does not currently expose an Air 15" 2024 M3 family label, so the shared map does not guess.

A3240.conflict_resolved: listings-audit previously used the wrong A3211 placeholder for MacBook Air 13-inch M4, and bm-grade-check previously reused A3114. Apple identifies the 13-inch M4 Air as A3240, which maps cleanly to the V7 family label Air 13" 2025 M4.

A2289.conflict_resolved: bm-grade-check hard-coded the non-canonical V7 family string "Pro 13\" 2020 Intel", but sell-prices-latest.json exposes no Intel 13-inch Pro family labels. The shared map preserves the A-number and blocks stale scraper fallback.

A2338.conflict_resolved: Apple uses A2338 for both MacBook Pro 13-inch M1 (2020) and MacBook Pro 13-inch M2 (2022). Because V7 exposes two exact family labels for the same A-number, bm-grade-check no longer guesses; listings-audit uses the shared candidate list to resolve title patterns without duplicating A-number literals.

A2779.conflict_resolved: Apple's A2779 chassis spans M2 Pro and M2 Max builds, but sell-prices-latest.json exposes a single canonical V7 family label for this generation: Pro 14" 2023 M2 Pro. The shared map standardises on the exact V7 label.

A2780.conflict_resolved: Apple's A2780 chassis spans M2 Pro and M2 Max builds, but sell-prices-latest.json exposes a single canonical V7 family label for this generation: Pro 16" 2023 M2 Pro. The shared map standardises on the exact V7 label.

A2918.conflict_resolved: bm-grade-check incorrectly mapped A2918 to Pro 14" 2023 M3 Pro. Apple identifies A2918 as the base 14-inch M3 model, and the exact V7 family label is Pro 14" 2023 M3.

A2991.conflict_resolved: bm-grade-check incorrectly mapped A2991 to Pro 14" 2023 M3. Apple identifies A2991 as the 16-inch 2023 M3 Pro/Max chassis, and sell-prices-latest.json exposes the canonical family label Pro 16" 2023 M3 Pro for that generation.

A2992.conflict_resolved: The repo previously crossed A2918, A2991, and A2992. Apple identifies A2992 as the 14-inch 2023 M3 Pro/Max chassis; sell-prices-latest.json exposes the canonical family label Pro 14" 2023 M3 Pro, so the shared map uses that exact V7 label.
